issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the dropdown menu controller adds the “open” CSS class and shows the panel, but it never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance` after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel and removes the “open” class, yet it never sets `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, so assistive technologies cannot detect the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_closeInstance` after the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns are registered, the code sets `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑controls` but does not initialise `aria‑expanded` (should be `"false"`). Without this, the accessibility tree lacks the required state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In the `register` function, after setting other ARIA attributes, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module’s `_syncToggleState` toggles visual classes for the billing‑period switch but never updates `aria‑pressed` on the button, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the annual option is active.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling the visual state, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates prices but does not provide an audible announcement of the new billing period, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element, so the hidden navigation remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions emit custom events but do not use a live region to announce that the navigation menu has opened or closed, which is required for users relying on auditory feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` emit custom events but do not provide live‑region announcements, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback when a submenu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not set aria‑expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open` and `close`, the code animates the panel but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the question button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern and leaving assistive technology unaware of the state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`; in `close`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region (duplicate of Issue 5 but for completeness in a separate sweep)
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates price displays without informing users of the new billing cycle via a live region, violating JS‑C.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period set to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label);` after the UI updates.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not override this rule, blocking keyboard users from seeing their focus position.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding the error state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false". Invalid state may persist in the accessibility tree even after the user corrects the input.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the answer is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function reveals the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the hamburger trigger. Assistive technology users will not know the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function shows the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Users relying on screen readers will not receive the state change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle does not update aria-checked or aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates visual classes for the billing period toggle but does not update aria-checked (for role="switch") or aria-pressed. The state change is invisible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visible state of the navigation but do not announce the change via a live region. Users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle visibility but do not announce the state change. Users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: Product filter application not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the grid and count badge but does not announce the filter change or new result count via a live region. Users are not informed that the content has updated.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and toggle state but does not announce the billing period change. Users are not informed that prices have switched to monthly or annual.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions expand/collapse answers but do not announce the action. Users are not informed when an answer is shown or hidden.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and _close functions toggle the suggestion list but do not update aria-expanded on the search input. Combobox pattern requires this attribute to reflect the list visibility.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button on page load. The initial closed state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger on initialization. The initial closed state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually. Screen-reader-only text should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clip properties to hide visually while remaining accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion, which can cause vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by toggling the 'hidden' attribute and CSS classes, but it does not synchronise the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern and leaves assistive technology users unaware of the panel's visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance after inst.open = true): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line (in _closeInstance after inst.open = false): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the function updates the visual state and animates the panel height, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close() function sets it to "false", the open() function must set it to "true" to maintain the correct accessibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open() after item.open = true): setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing-period toggle buttons (monthly, annual, etc.) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes, but they do not expose the pressed state to assistive technologies via aria-pressed. Users cannot tell which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _syncToggleState forEach loop): setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: pricing switch control missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle switch element is assigned role="switch" but the code never updates the aria-checked attribute to reflect the on/off state (year vs month). Switch components must communicate their state via aria-checked, not aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _onToggleClick after calculating next): setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, next === 'year');
  Line (in init after setting initial period): setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, initPeriod === 'year');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate that the drawer is initially closed. This missing initial state can confuse screen-reader users before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _initialised = true): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element acts as a combobox that controls the suggestion listbox, but it is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the listbox is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _initialised = true): setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, the price values update visually with animation, but screen-reader users are not informed that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in setPeriod after updating plan cards): liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live-region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ answer updates the visual state and URL hash, but does not announce the change to screen-reader users via the live region. Users may not realise the answer is now visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open() after opening panel): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);
  Line (in close() after closing panel): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : `${item.question} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live-region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visual state and traps focus (or should), but the action is not announced to screen-reader users. Users may not realise the menu is now open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open() after opening): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);
  Line (in close() after closing): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name assurance
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM and event listeners are attached, but the JavaScript does not ensure the button has an accessible name via aria-label. If the HTML element lacks text content or an aria-label, the button will be announced as "button" with no label.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _initialised = true): if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus should be trapped within the drawer to prevent keyboard users from tabbing into the background content. The code declares a _trap variable but never initialises or activates a focus trap when the drawer opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open() after opening drawer): if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips missing live-region announcement on removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes an active filter chip, the filter is cleared and the product grid updates, but no announcement is made to screen-reader users indicating that the filter has been removed and what the new filter state is.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _onChipClick after calling productFilter methods): liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed. Showing ${productFilter.getState().activeCategory} products.`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live-region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu updates the panel visibility, but the state change is not announced to screen-reader users, particularly when the menu is closed and focus returns to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.explanation: The nav element inside the MobileNav dialog does not have an aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish this secondary navigation landmark from other navigation regions on the page.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos used for the seamless marquee loop is not hidden with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the list of partners twice, which creates a repetitive and confusing experience.

issuesFound.title: StarRating component missing role img
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component's container span provides an aria-label describing the rating value but lacks role="img", which is necessary to ensure the entire rating is treated as a single image-like entity with the accessible name, rather than individual text elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 569: <span aria-label={`${labelPrefix}: ${value} out of ${max} stars`} role="img">
